Hover text lets you see a larger view of the item under the pointer when you hover over something. You can choose a short-key, and when you press the short key, the text under the pointer will be enlarged. You can adjust the size, color, background color, border color, and font of the text that is enlarged.

Hover typing is a function that display words in larger text while you're typing. It appears as a larger, dark, input box with larger text once you start typing with your device. You can adjust the box's position to the top of your screen or around the texting input. You can also adjust the hover typing box's display and color. It helps you see what you're typing in bigger size, or highlight misspelled and autocorrected words.

If you have trouble where multiple copies of the same character show up if you don't remove your finger from the key quickly enough you can use this feature to increase the time before key repeat starts.  (Also sometimes called  "RepeatKeys".)

DESCRIPTION: Spoken Content is a feature found on iPadOS, iOS, and macOS that allows you to select text and have it read aloud to you. You can hear the entire screen or select a specific section. You can also hear what you type spoken out loud, character by character or word by word.

When you are using an external keyboard, you can use this feature allow typing with one hand/finger/headstick.  When this feature is turned on the modifier keys (Shift, CTRL, Option, CMD) become sticky.  If you press a modifier key (with StickyKeys turned on) the modifer key will "stick" down until you type a non-modifer key.  You can press serveral modifier keys in a row then a non-modifier key and all modifierkeys will be applied to the non-modifier key when it is pressed.   The modifier keys are automatically released after a non-modifier key is pressed.    If you want to lock down a modifierkey - press it twice in a row.    To release a modifier key you can press it another time.
